slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 3 - Computer Science
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'allows an I/O device to transfer data directly to or from the system memory without passing through the CPU.'?
options: Set Theory Operations
Direct Memory Access (DMA)
Group Theory Group
Full Adder
slides.explanation: Correct! **Direct Memory Access (DMA)** is defined as: allows an I/O device to transfer data directly to or from the system memory without passing through the CPU. (cpu-free I/O-memory transfer).

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'are situations in CPU execution (structural, data, control) that prevent the next instruction from executing in its designated cycle.'?
options: Equivalence Relation
Permutation
Flip-Flops
Pipelining Hazards
slides.explanation: Correct! **Pipelining Hazards** is defined as: are situations in CPU execution (structural, data, control) that prevent the next instruction from executing in its designated cycle. (instruction pipeline execution stalls).

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'consists of the sequence of actions: Fetch, Decode, Read effective address, Execute, and Write-back.'?
options: Synchronous Counter
Partially Ordered Set (Poset)
Instruction Cycle
Boolean Algebra Minimization
slides.explanation: Correct! **Instruction Cycle** is defined as: consists of the sequence of actions: Fetch, Decode, Read effective address, Execute, and Write-back. (fetch-decode-execute basic loop).

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'features a small set of simple, uniform, single-cycle execution instructions to optimize compiler efficiency.'?
options: Decoder
RISC Architecture
Karnaugh Map (K-map)
Vertex Degree
slides.explanation: Correct! **RISC Architecture** is defined as: features a small set of simple, uniform, single-cycle execution instructions to optimize compiler efficiency. (reduced uniform instruction set design).

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the widely adopted technical standard for floating-point computation, defining sign, exponent, and mantissa formats.'?
options: Multiplexer (MUX)
Von Neumann Architecture
IEEE 754 Standard
Euler Path
slides.explanation: Correct! **IEEE 754 Standard** is defined as: is the widely adopted technical standard for floating-point computation, defining sign, exponent, and mantissa formats. (floating-point binary representation format).

slides.question: Q6: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Propositional Logic Connectives**?
options: Its primary focus is one-to-many signal router.
Its primary focus is multi-level fast memory staging.
Its primary focus is compound proposition builders.
Its primary focus is single-visit vertex traversal loop.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Propositional Logic Connectives** is: **compound proposition builders**.

slides.question: Q7: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Predicate Logic Quantifiers**?
options: Its primary focus is closure-associativity-identity-inverse algebraic structure.
Its primary focus is predicate variable scoping symbols.
Its primary focus is three-input binary addition circuit.
Its primary focus is cpu-free I/O-memory transfer.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Predicate Logic Quantifiers** is: **predicate variable scoping symbols**.

slides.question: Q8: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Set Theory Operations**?
options: Its primary focus is set manipulation mathematical operations.
Its primary focus is order-sensitive selection arrangement.
Its primary focus is clock-driven sequential memory components.
Its primary focus is instruction pipeline execution stalls.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Set Theory Operations** is: **set manipulation mathematical operations**.

slides.question: Q9: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Equivalence Relation**?
options: Its primary focus is simultaneously-clocked state counter.
Its primary focus is partitioning reflexive-symmetric-transitive relation.
Its primary focus is fetch-decode-execute basic loop.
Its primary focus is logic gate reduction optimization.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Equivalence Relation** is: **partitioning reflexive-symmetric-transitive relation**.

slides.question: Q10: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Partially Ordered Set (Poset)**?
options: Its primary focus is reflexive-antisymmetric-transitive ordering framework.
Its primary focus is reduced uniform instruction set design.
Its primary focus is graphical boolean simplification table.
Its primary focus is binary-to-unary line converter.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Partially Ordered Set (Poset)** is: **reflexive-antisymmetric-transitive ordering framework**.

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'minimizes the kernel size by moving non-essential services (like file systems, drivers) into user space.'?
options: Microkernel Architecture
Process Synchronization
Inter-Process Communication (IPC)
Page Replacement Algorithm
slides.explanation: Correct! **Microkernel Architecture** is defined as: minimizes the kernel size by moving non-essential services (like file systems, drivers) into user space. (minimal kernel user-space service design).

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'ensure that data changes in one local cache are correctly propagated to other caches in a multiprocessor system.'?
options: Deadlock Prevention
Thrashing
Cache Coherence Protocols
I/O Buffering
slides.explanation: Correct! **Cache Coherence Protocols** is defined as: ensure that data changes in one local cache are correctly propagated to other caches in a multiprocessor system. (multiprocessor cache synchronization).

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a memory management technique that divides virtual memory into variable-sized segments based on logical modules.'?
options: Disk Scheduling
Thread
Banker's Algorithm
Memory Segmentation
slides.explanation: Correct! **Memory Segmentation** is defined as: is a memory management technique that divides virtual memory into variable-sized segments based on logical modules. (logical module-based memory division).

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a specialized software program that controls a specific hardware device attached to the computer.'?
options: File Allocation Methods
Paging
Mutex
Device Driver
slides.explanation: Correct! **Device Driver** is defined as: is a specialized software program that controls a specific hardware device attached to the computer. (hardware-specific control translator program).

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is an acronym for Simultaneous Peripheral Operations On-Line, storing printer/device jobs in a buffer to execute sequentially.'?
options: Spooling
Counting Semaphore
System Calls
Virtual Memory
slides.explanation: Correct! **Spooling** is defined as: is an acronym for Simultaneous Peripheral Operations On-Line, storing printer/device jobs in a buffer to execute sequentially. (peripheral device print job spooler).

slides.question: Q16: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Process States**?
options: Its primary focus is cpu state saving-restoring overhead.
Its primary focus is process lifecycle stage indicators.
Its primary focus is fast page table lookup cache.
Its primary focus is redundancy-performance disk array configurations.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Process States** is: **process lifecycle stage indicators**.

slides.question: Q17: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **CPU Scheduling**?
options: Its primary focus is physical memory page eviction policy.
Its primary focus is minimal kernel user-space service design.
Its primary focus is ready queue CPU allocation.
Its primary focus is isolated process communication mechanisms.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **CPU Scheduling** is: **ready queue CPU allocation**.

slides.question: Q18: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Process Synchronization**?
options: Its primary focus is multiprocessor cache synchronization.
Its primary focus is speed-matching data storage buffer.
Its primary focus is prevention of race conditions.
Its primary focus is excessive paging performance collapse.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Process Synchronization** is: **prevention of race conditions**.

slides.question: Q19: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Deadlock Prevention**?
options: Its primary focus is lightweight shared-memory execution unit.
Its primary focus is coffman condition elimination strategy.
Its primary focus is logical module-based memory division.
Its primary focus is hard drive head seek optimization.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Deadlock Prevention** is: **coffman condition elimination strategy**.

slides.question: Q20: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Banker's Algorithm**?
options: Its primary focus is safe-state resource allocation simulator.
Its primary focus is physical disk block mapping strategies.
Its primary focus is single-thread resource lock.
Its primary focus is hardware-specific control translator program.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Banker's Algorithm** is: **safe-state resource allocation simulator**.

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is an OOP mechanism where a new class (derived) adopts the attributes and behaviors of an existing class (base).'?
options: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
Doubly Linked List
Inheritance
Dynamic Programming
slides.explanation: Correct! **Inheritance** is defined as: is an OOP mechanism where a new class (derived) adopts the attributes and behaviors of an existing class (base). (derived-to-base class reuse mechanism).

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the OOP practice of bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit, restricting direct access.'?
options: Encapsulation
Merge Sort
Depth-First Search (DFS)
Hashing
slides.explanation: Correct! **Encapsulation** is defined as: is the OOP practice of bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit, restricting direct access. (data-hiding and modular unit bundling).

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a mathematical model for data types defined by its behavior (operations) from the point of view of a user, not implementation.'?
options: Heap Data Structure
Binary Search
Big O Complexity
Abstract Data Type (ADT)
slides.explanation: Correct! **Abstract Data Type (ADT)** is defined as: is a mathematical model for data types defined by its behavior (operations) from the point of view of a user, not implementation. (mathematical operation behavior model).

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is an automatic memory management process that reclaims heap memory occupied by objects that are no longer in use.'?
options: Stack Data Structure
Call Stack
Garbage Collection
AVL Tree
slides.explanation: Correct! **Garbage Collection** is defined as: is an automatic memory management process that reclaims heap memory occupied by objects that are no longer in use. (automatic dead heap memory reclamation).

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'defines the region of program source code text within which a variable's binding or name is valid and accessible.'?
options: Variable Scope
Queue Data Structure
Quick Sort
Pointer dereferencing
slides.explanation: Correct! **Variable Scope** is defined as: defines the region of program source code text within which a variable's binding or name is valid and accessible. (name accessibility region code boundary).

slides.question: Q26: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Binary Search Tree (BST)**?
options: Its primary focus is single interface multi-implementation capability.
Its primary focus is one-way pointer node sequence.
Its primary focus is space-efficient sparse graph representation.
Its primary focus is O(log n) average search time.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Binary Search Tree (BST)** is: **O(log n) average search time**.

slides.question: Q27: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Dijkstra's Algorithm**?
options: Its primary focus is shortest path in unweighted graphs.
Its primary focus is single-source shortest path optimization.
Its primary focus is two-way pointer node sequence.
Its primary focus is derived-to-base class reuse mechanism.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Dijkstra's Algorithm** is: **single-source shortest path optimization**.

slides.question: Q28: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Dynamic Programming**?
options: Its primary focus is memoization and tabulation paradigm.
Its primary focus is O(n log n) stable comparison sort.
Its primary focus is data-hiding and modular unit bundling.
Its primary focus is connectivity and topological sorting tool.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Dynamic Programming** is: **memoization and tabulation paradigm**.

slides.question: Q29: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Hashing**?
options: Its primary focus is asymptotic run-time upper bound indicator.
Its primary focus is O(log n) sorted array search.
Its primary focus is O(1) average lookup complexity.
Its primary focus is mathematical operation behavior model.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Hashing** is: **O(1) average lookup complexity**.

slides.question: Q30: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Heap Data Structure**?
options: Its primary focus is efficient priority queue implementation.
Its primary focus is active subroutine memory tracker.
Its primary focus is automatic dead heap memory reclamation.
Its primary focus is LIFO element storage collection.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Heap Data Structure** is: **efficient priority queue implementation**.

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a precompiled collection of SQL statements stored in the database, reducing network traffic and improving performance.'?
options: Lost Update Anomaly
Third Normal Form (3NF)
Stored Procedure
SQL JOIN
slides.explanation: Correct! **Stored Procedure** is defined as: is a precompiled collection of SQL statements stored in the database, reducing network traffic and improving performance. (precompiled database SQL package).

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a column or group of columns that uniquely identifies each row in a database table, and cannot contain NULL values.'?
options: Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)
Relational Algebra
Primary Key
Dirty Read
slides.explanation: Correct! **Primary Key** is defined as: is a column or group of columns that uniquely identifies each row in a database table, and cannot contain NULL values. (row unique identifier constraint).

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a minimal superkey that can uniquely identify any database record without redundant attributes.'?
options: Candidate Key
Database View
Non-Repeatable Read
ACID Properties
slides.explanation: Correct! **Candidate Key** is defined as: is a minimal superkey that can uniquely identify any database record without redundant attributes. (minimal row identifier set).

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a state where all foreign key values in a database are valid, pointing to existing primary keys in parent tables.'?
options: Referential Integrity
Phantom Read
NoSQL Database
Foreign Key
slides.explanation: Correct! **Referential Integrity** is defined as: is a state where all foreign key values in a database are valid, pointing to existing primary keys in parent tables. (valid foreign-to-primary key reference state).

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'occurs when the same piece of data is stored in two or more separate places in a database unnecessarily.'?
options: Data Redundancy
ER Modeling
B+ Tree Index
Query Optimizer
slides.explanation: Correct! **Data Redundancy** is defined as: occurs when the same piece of data is stored in two or more separate places in a database unnecessarily. (unnecessary duplicate data storage).

slides.question: Q36: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **First Normal Form (1NF)**?
options: Its primary focus is reverting uncommitted transactional modifications.
Its primary focus is serializability guarantee in transactions.
Its primary focus is automatic event-driven code block.
Its primary focus is atomic column values requirement.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **First Normal Form (1NF)** is: **atomic column values requirement**.

slides.question: Q37: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Second Normal Form (2NF)**?
options: Its primary focus is concurrent write overwrite anomaly.
Its primary focus is precompiled database SQL package.
Its primary focus is multi-table data retrieval operation.
Its primary focus is elimination of partial dependencies.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Second Normal Form (2NF)** is: **elimination of partial dependencies**.

slides.question: Q38: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Third Normal Form (3NF)**?
options: Its primary focus is reading uncommitted transaction modifications.
Its primary focus is elimination of transitive dependencies.
Its primary focus is row unique identifier constraint.
Its primary focus is theoretical query language foundation.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Third Normal Form (3NF)** is: **elimination of transitive dependencies**.

slides.question: Q39: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)**?
options: Its primary focus is reading committed concurrent modifications.
Its primary focus is virtual table query abstraction.
Its primary focus is eliminating overlapping candidate key anomalies.
Its primary focus is minimal row identifier set.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)** is: **eliminating overlapping candidate key anomalies**.

slides.question: Q40: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **ACID Properties**?
options: Its primary focus is non-relational schema-less storage.
Its primary focus is valid foreign-to-primary key reference state.
Its primary focus is reading committed concurrent insertions.
Its primary focus is transaction reliability standards.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **ACID Properties** is: **transaction reliability standards**.

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'measures the degree of interdependence between different software modules, aiming for a low level to increase modularity.'?
options: CI/CD Pipeline
Subnet Mask
Coupling
Firewall
slides.explanation: Correct! **Coupling** is defined as: measures the degree of interdependence between different software modules, aiming for a low level to increase modularity. (inter-module interdependence indicator).

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a structured process used by the software industry to design, develop, and test high-quality software (e.g., Waterfall).'?
options: SDLC Model
IPv6 Address Space
Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)
Black-box Testing
slides.explanation: Correct! **SDLC Model** is defined as: is a structured process used by the software industry to design, develop, and test high-quality software (e.g., Waterfall). (software development process model).

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'provide a standardized visual representation of software systems to describe classes, objects, and their relationships.'?
options: White-box Testing
UML Diagrams
Symmetric Encryption
Agile Methodology
slides.explanation: Correct! **UML Diagrams** is defined as: provide a standardized visual representation of software systems to describe classes, objects, and their relationships. (standardized software visualization diagrams).

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a formal document describing the functional and non-functional requirements that the software system must fulfill.'?
options: Asymmetric Encryption
SRS Document
Software Architecture
Design Pattern
slides.explanation: Correct! **SRS Document** is defined as: is a formal document describing the functional and non-functional requirements that the software system must fulfill. (formal software requirements specifications).

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the process of restructuring existing computer code without changing its external behavior, to improve readability and structure.'?
options: Unit Testing
Scrum Framework
DNS (Domain Name System)
Refactoring
slides.explanation: Correct! **Refactoring** is defined as: is the process of restructuring existing computer code without changing its external behavior, to improve readability and structure. (improving code quality without behavior change).

slides.question: Q46: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **OSI Model**?
options: Its primary focus is distributed source control management.
Its primary focus is single module functional focus score.
Its primary focus is 7-layer abstraction framework.
Its primary focus is IP to MAC address resolver.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **OSI Model** is: **7-layer abstraction framework**.

slides.question: Q47: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **TCP/IP Protocol**?
options: Its primary focus is reliable internet transport framework.
Its primary focus is inter-module interdependence indicator.
Its primary focus is traffic filtering barrier.
Its primary focus is automated deployment automation.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **TCP/IP Protocol** is: **reliable internet transport framework**.

slides.question: Q48: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Subnet Mask**?
options: Its primary focus is 128-bit internet address space standard.
Its primary focus is functional specification validation.
Its primary focus is software development process model.
Its primary focus is IP address segmentation tool.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Subnet Mask** is: **IP address segmentation tool**.

slides.question: Q49: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)**?
options: Its primary focus is standardized software visualization diagrams.
Its primary focus is internal code path validation.
Its primary focus is flexible iterative development paradigm.
Its primary focus is internet autonomous routing protocol.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)** is: **internet autonomous routing protocol**.

slides.question: Q50: In computer systems, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Symmetric Encryption**?
options: Its primary focus is reusable architectural solution blueprint.
Its primary focus is formal software requirements specifications.
Its primary focus is single shared-key cryptography.
Its primary focus is high-level structural system organization.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Symmetric Encryption** is: **single shared-key cryptography**.

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 3. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Computer Science Mock Exam 3.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Computer Science questions!
